items.question: A column `risk_band` has levels low, medium, high with a true order. Which encoding is appropriate for logistic regression?
items.explanation: Ordered bands map naturally to ranked integers; one-hot also works but ordinal is valid when steps are meaningful.

items.question: A `city` column has 12,000 unique values. What is the most practical first alternative to full one-hot?
options: Target encoding with cross-fitted means and smoothing
items.answer: Target encoding with cross-fitted means and smoothing
items.explanation: High cardinality makes one-hot costly; leakage-safe target encoding with smoothing compresses levels into one numeric feature.

items.question: Why should `df.groupby('store')['sales'].transform('mean')` not be used directly as a feature on the same rows?
options: It leaks each row's target into its own feature value
items.answer: It leaks each row's target into its own feature value
items.explanation: In-sample category means include the current row's label—classic target leakage.

items.question: `payment_type` is nominal (cash, card, wallet). What encoding avoids false ordering in linear regression?
items.explanation: Nominal types have no rank; one-hot gives separate coefficients without implying card is twice cash.

items.question: An unseen category appears at inference after `OneHotEncoder(handle_unknown='ignore')`. What happens?
options: All dummy columns for that feature are zero
The model auto-creates a new column
items.answer: All dummy columns for that feature are zero
items.explanation: Unknown levels produce an all-zero vector—distinct from any known level unless you map to an explicit unknown bucket.
